revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[FIFA]], [[IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[FIFA]], [[IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[FIFA]], [[IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[:en:IOC|IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[:en:United States Coast Guard|United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[:en:IOC|IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[:en:United States Coast Guard|United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[:en:IOC|IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[:en:United States Coast Guard|United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[:en:IOC|IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[:en:United States Coast Guard|United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[:en:IOC|IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[:en:United States Coast Guard|United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[:en:IOC|IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[:en:United States Coast Guard|United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[:en:IOC|IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[:en:United States Coast Guard|United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[:en:IOC|IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23176857634|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[:en:United States Coast Guard|United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[:en:IOC|IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[:en:United States Coast Guard|United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[:en:IOC|IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Coast_guard%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} This template is used to display a small flag variant icon next to a wikilinked name of a country's coastguard. It is a usable wrapper of the {{tlp|Coast guard/core}} template, which should not be accessed directly. It is built on the {{tlp|flag}} template. == Usage == '''{{Coast guard|'''''country identifier'''''|'''''optional variant'''''|'''name=''alternative text string'''''|'''size=''maximum width and/or height of the flag image'''''}}''' * ''country identifier'' is the common name of the country (e.g. "United States"), a common alias (e.g. "US"), or a standard country code such as those listed at [[:en:ISO 3166-1 alpha-3|ISO 3166-1 alpha-3]] * ''variant'' is an optional second parameter that can be used to display a coastguard flag variation. The list of variants for each country is documented on the appropriate template page, such as [[:Template:Country data Germany]]. You can only use subvariants of the coast guard variant. * ''alternative text string'', this optional value, specified by the '''name''' parameter, is used to display alternative text instead of the official coast guard name for the wikilinked page. * ''size'', this optional value is specified by the '''size''' parameter, and is used to specify the maximum width and/or height of the flag image. This parameter is specified using standard [[Wikipedia:Extended image syntax|image syntax]]. ==Examples== {| class="wikitable" ! Wiki markup !! Displays !! Notes |- | {{Coast guard|United States}} || {{nowrap| {{Coast guard|United States}}}} || rowspan=2 | Note that both instances link to [[:en:United States Coast Guard|United States Coast Guard]]. |- | {{Coast guard|USA}} || {{Coast guard|USA}} |- | {{Coast guard|Germany}} || {{Coast guard|Germany}} || rowspan=3 | All three instances link to [[:en:German Federal Coast Guard|German Federal Coast Guard]].
In addition to the ISO country codes, those used by [[:en:FIFA|FIFA]], [[:en:IOC|IOC]], etc. are also supported. |- | {{Coast guard|DEU}} || {{Coast guard|DEU}} |- | {{Coast guard|GER}} || {{Coast guard|GER}} |- | {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || {{Coast guard|CAN|name=Canadian}} || The name parameter can be used to change the display string but keep the link to the correct article. |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=14px}} || rowspan=2 | The size parameter can be used to control the image dimensions.
23×15px is the default size for most countries ([[:Category:Country data templates with distinct default size|some entities]] have a different default size). |- | {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} || {{Coast guard|USA|size=28px}} |} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "description": "This template is used to display a small flag icon next to a wikilinked name of that country's coastguard", "params": { "1": { "label": "Country identifier", "description": "Common name of the country (e.g. 'United States'), a common alias (e.g. 'US'), or a standard country code", "type": "string", "required": true }, "2": { "label": "Variant", "description": "Identifies a coastguard flag variant to be used instead of the standard flag", "type": "string", "required": false }, "name": { "label": "Alternative text", "description": "Display alternative text instead of the standard country coastguard name", "type": "string", "required": false }, "size": { "label": "Maximum dimension", "description": "The maximum width or height, specified via standard image syntax (e.g. x30px)", "type": "string", "required": false } } } == See Also == See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Flag Template]] for other flag-based templates. {{sandbox other|| }} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Flag template system]] [[Category:Military templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
